title: The Grocery Store Meltdown

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I'll never forget the grocery store. <break time="350ms"/>Sofia was three. <break time="300ms"/>Full meltdown in aisle seven.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I mean screaming. <break time="300ms"/>On the floor. <break time="250ms"/>Kicking. <break time="400ms"/>All because I said no to a cereal box. <break time="350ms"/>And this woman - <break time="300ms"/>older, perfect hair, judgmental face - <break time="250ms"/>walked by and said, <break time="200ms"/>'Someone needs discipline.' <break time="500ms"/>I stood there. <break time="400ms"/>Sofia screaming. <break time="350ms"/>My face burning. <break time="300ms"/>And I just... <break time="250ms"/>left the cart. <break time="200ms"/>Picked her up. <break time="150ms"/>Walked out. <break time="400ms"/>I cried in the parking lot for twenty minutes.
story.reflection: <break time="400ms"/>Here's what I know now. <break time="350ms"/>That woman? <break time="300ms"/>She didn't matter. <break time="250ms"/>She didn't know my daughter. <break time="200ms"/>She didn't know me. <break time="400ms"/>She didn't know that Sofia had barely slept. <break time="350ms"/>That I was running on empty. <break time="300ms"/>That I was doing my best. <break time="500ms"/>Every parent has an aisle seven. <break time="400ms"/>And anyone who judges you in yours <break time="350ms"/>has forgotten their own.

when_to_share: When someone feels judged for their child's behavior in public or by others
